name: Jonathan Mingo
headline: Mingo's collapse leaves nothing for fantasy to chase
body:
The arrow points straight down. Mingo's positional finish has cratered from 76th in 2023 to 124th in 2024 to 194th this past season, when his role evaporated to a 20.7% snap share across just five games. He drew five targets total, an 0.8% team share, and converted them into one catch for 25 yards. His -0.84 EPA per play and 20% success rate weren't small-sample noise so much as confirmation of a 2024 that already bottomed out at a 28.6% success rate.

The context is just as bleak. He sits at the back of a crowded Dallas receiver room with no path to meaningful snaps, and a restructured deal stripping his workout bonus signals how the team values him. He's effectively unrostered league-wide and projects for minimal volume. There is no reason to draft him in any standard format.
